conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: We need to pick a database for the new inventory service. I'm looking at Postgres, MongoDB, and DynamoDB.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My upstairs neighbor had some furniture delivered today, lots of noise.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop update took forever to install last night.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Postgres is nice because most of the team already knows SQL well.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The weather's been unusually warm for this time of year.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Mongo's flexible schema might save us some pain early on since the data model isn't fully settled yet.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The elevator in my building has been out of service since Monday.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Also, the office is doing a potluck on Friday, I need to figure out what to bring.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally returned that overdue library book.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: DynamoDB would mean one less thing to operate since we're already on AWS, but the query patterns are pretty restrictive.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to schedule a haircut, it's overdue at this point.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I keep going back and forth between Postgres and Mongo honestly.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally got around to replacing the batteries in the smoke detectors at home.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My sister called to catch up, hadn't talked in a few weeks.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Postgres does have some JSON support that might close the gap with Mongo's flexibility.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Still not 100% sure though, the inventory data does have a lot of nested structure.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My car's tire pressure light came on this morning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The smoke detector chirping was driving me crazy for like two weeks before I fixed it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My favorite coffee shop is testing a new seasonal drink.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I looked at how another team handled a similar inventory model and they seemed happy with a relational setup.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: DynamoDB's pricing model is appealing but I don't think our access patterns are predictable enough for it to pay off yet.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The package I ordered got delayed by a couple of days.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I also need to schedule my car for an oil change soon.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Leaning more towards Postgres at this point but want to sleep on it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The potluck got moved to next Friday instead, apparently.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: After going back and forth, we've decided on Postgres for the inventory service. The team's SQL experience plus JSONB columns for the flexible parts of the schema tipped it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Good, I'll start setting up the migrations for it next week.

query: What database did the team decide to use for the inventory service?
